description: Centralized Python virtual environment manager — pyenv-style workflow powered by uv. Written in Rust.
rules: scoop is installed via `cargo install scoop-uv`. The binary name is `scoop`, the crate name is `scoop-uv`.
Configuration file is at ~/.scoop/config.json (JSON format, NOT TOML). Never reference ~/.config/scoop/config.toml.
Virtual environments are stored centrally in ~/.scoop/virtualenvs/.
Shell integration uses the eval pattern (like pyenv): `eval "$(scoop init zsh)"`.
Supported shells: bash, zsh, fish, powershell.
Supported languages: en, ko, ja, pt-BR. Set via `scoop lang <code>` or SCOOP_LANG env var.
Version file priority: SCOOP_VERSION env > .scoop-version (local + parent walk) > ~/.scoop/version (global).
To set a specific Python version globally, create an environment with that version, then run `scoop use <env> --global`.
`scoop use --global` writes an environment name to ~/.scoop/version, not a raw Python version string.
To create a new environment with an exact Python version (for example 3.9.5), use `scoop install 3.9.5` then `scoop create <name> 3.9.5`.
Verify environment Python with `scoop info <name>`. If version resolution fails, inspect `uv python list` and `scoop list --pythons`.
To remove a Python version and all associated environments, use `scoop uninstall <version> --cascade` (add `--force` for non-interactive use).
Without `--cascade`, uninstalling Python does not delete dependent environments and they may become broken; verify cleanup with `scoop list --pythons` and `scoop doctor`.
For temporary auto-activation disable without global impact, use `export SCOOP_NO_AUTO=1` in the current shell and `unset SCOOP_NO_AUTO` when done.
For project-specific behavior, use local `.scoop-version` via `scoop use <env>` or `scoop use system`; for terminal-only override use `scoop shell <env|system>` and `scoop shell --unset`.
Once an environment is active, install dependencies from requirements file with `pip install -r requirements.txt`.
For non-root dependency files, use `pip install -r path/to/requirements.txt`; verify installed packages with `pip list`.
To inspect managed versions and associations: `scoop list --pythons` (versions), `scoop list` (envs), and `scoop list --python-version <VERSION>` (envs for one version).
Use `--json` for machine-readable output and `--bare` for script-friendly output when listing versions or environments.

If required Python is unavailable in default sources, integrate custom interpreters via `scoop create <env> --python-path /path/to/python` or add interpreter to PATH then run `scoop create <env> <version>`.
Verify custom integration using `uv python list`, `scoop info <env>`, and `scoop doctor -v`; custom interpreter path is stored in `.scoop-metadata.json` (`python_path`).
.python-version is NOT supported. Use .scoop-version for version pinning.
Environment names must match ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$ (max 64 chars, must start with letter).
Most commands support --json for machine-readable output.
MSRV is 1.85 (Rust Edition 2024). N-1 MSRV policy.
Project manifest file is .scoop.toml (parent-walk discovery from cwd). Defines [environment].python and [packages].default + named groups; reconcile the active env with the manifest via `scoop sync`.
To run a one-off command in an env without activating it, use `scoop run <env> -- <cmd>` (e.g. `scoop run myproject -- pytest tests/`). The `--` separator is required before the command.
To inspect current state use `scoop status` (Active/Configured/System/None) and `scoop which <exe>` (resolves an executable inside the active env).

For collaboration, snapshot an env with `scoop export <name> > env.json` (schema v1) and restore with `scoop import env.json --name <new>`. To duplicate locally, use `scoop clone <src> <dst>` (add `--no-packages` for a skeleton).
Lazy Python install: `scoop create <name> <version> --install-python` installs the requested Python via uv if missing, then creates the env.
`scoop migrate all` runs in parallel via rayon; `--dry-run` and single-env (`migrate @env`) stay sequential.
To free disk space from the uv download/wheel cache, run `scoop prune` (thin wrapper around `uv cache prune`).

To clean up orphan virtualenvs (missing metadata or broken Python), run `scoop gc` (default is preview/dry-run). Pass `-y`/`--yes` to actually remove. `--aggressive` also flags uv-managed Python versions that no environment references.

To diagnose env health, run `scoop verify [NAME]`. Runs 6 checks per env (metadata, python binary, pyvenv.cfg, activate script, python executes, .scoop.toml drift). Default always exits 0; pass `--strict` to exit 1 on any issue for CI gates. Use `verify` for per-env diagnosis, `doctor` for system-level, `gc` to remove orphans.
To generate man pages, use `scoop man` (stdout, pipe to `man -l -`) or `scoop man <DIR>` (writes `scoop.1` + `scoop-<sub>.1` files for distro packagers).
For complete LLM reference, see llms-full.txt in the repository root.
